project.name: simtalk
project.description: SimTalk is a realtime speech-to-speech translation web app built around OpenAI gpt-realtime-translate, with a pnpm workspace (React 19 + Vite frontend, Hono backend, Zod-shared types) that mints short-lived realtime credentials and LiveKit room tokens so the browser can run translated WebRTC sessions directly without server-side audio. Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

nodes.name: Icons.tsx
nodes.summary: Defines the SimTalk inline SVG icon set and the STIcon React component used as the brand-wide icon renderer across screens and primitives.

nodes.name: STIcon
nodes.summary: React component that renders one of the named SimTalk SVG icons at a configurable size and color, used as the canonical icon primitive across the UI.

nodes.name: LanguagePicker.tsx
nodes.summary: Modal language-picker sheet for selecting source/target languages, with a tappable card preview and a searchable list backed by the shared LANGUAGES catalog.

nodes.name: LangCard
nodes.summary: Tappable card that displays the currently selected language and opens the picker sheet when activated, used inside Lobby and other surfaces.

nodes.name: LanguagePickerSheet
nodes.summary: Full-height searchable language picker overlay with keyboard handling, search filtering, and selection callbacks; the primary UI for picking languages.

nodes.name: ModeSegmented.tsx
nodes.summary: Segmented control for switching between conversation modes (e.g. they-speak, you-speak, room) used at the top of the Lobby.

nodes.name: ModeSegmented
nodes.summary: Renders the SimTalk mode-selector pill row and emits onChange when the user picks a different conversation mode.

nodes.name: primitives.tsx
nodes.summary: Brand primitives module exporting SimTalk color tokens, fonts, gradient, and the core ST-prefixed UI primitives (button, card, title, chip, pill, live dot) used across every screen.

nodes.name: STButton
nodes.summary: Core SimTalk button primitive with variant, size, optional icon, and pressed-state styling; foundation for every action in the app.

nodes.name: STCard
nodes.summary: Branded card surface with tone variants and optional click handling, used to group content blocks across screens.

nodes.name: STTitle
nodes.summary: Display-font title component with configurable size, stroke, and drop shadow, used for the SimTalk hero headings.

nodes.name: STChip
nodes.summary: Compact labelled chip with optional icon and stroke used to display status, metadata, or category tags.

nodes.name: STPill
nodes.summary: Rounded pill component used for prominent status badges such as language tags and live state indicators.

nodes.name: LiveDot
nodes.summary: Animated dot indicator used to signal an active live session in headers and chips, respecting prefers-reduced-motion through its surrounding CSS.

nodes.name: AccessGateModal.tsx
nodes.summary: Modal dialog gating access to the app via an access code, with Escape-to-close, focus management, error display, and submission to the parent App.

nodes.name: AccessGateModal
nodes.summary: Renders the access-code modal, manages local input state, handles keyboard close, and submits trimmed codes back to the controlling App.

nodes.name: Lobby.tsx
nodes.summary: Lobby screen where the user picks mode, source and target languages, and either launches a translation session or creates a remote room.

nodes.name: Lobby
nodes.summary: Main Lobby React component that wires mode segmented control, language picker cards, swap action, launch button, and create-room button into the App's controllers.

nodes.name: PracticeSurface.tsx
nodes.summary: You-speak practice screen showing heard text, model translation, recording controls, transcribed attempt diffing and reveal/try-again/next phrase actions.

nodes.name: diff
nodes.summary: Tokenizes an attempt against a reference translation and returns per-token correctness metadata used to color the practice diff.

nodes.name: PracticeSurface
nodes.summary: Main practice React component coordinating recording state, attempt text, diff visualization and the reveal/try-again/next-phrase flow for you-speak mode.

nodes.name: TranscriptSheet.tsx
nodes.summary: Bottom-sheet style modal that lists translated transcript entries for the active session and offers a copy-all action.

nodes.name: TranscriptSheet
nodes.summary: Renders the transcript sheet overlay with Escape-to-close handling, a scrollable list of entries, and a copy-all callback for sharing the conversation.

nodes.name: DevDrawer.tsx
nodes.summary: Developer-only side drawer that surfaces the current realtime token metadata, session status, recording status and recent transcript deltas for debugging.

nodes.name: DevDrawer
nodes.summary: Renders the dev drawer overlay with Escape-to-close handling and human-friendly views of token expiry, session state and recent realtime deltas.

nodes.name: AccessGateModal.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Component tests for AccessGateModal covering rendering, error display, submission of trimmed codes, and close handling.

nodes.name: STButton.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Lightweight component test verifying the STButton primitive renders with expected label and accessible role.

nodes.name: accessGate.ts
nodes.summary: Browser sessionStorage helpers for the access-gate password plus an AccessDeniedError used to signal 401 responses from token endpoints.

nodes.name: getStoredPassword
nodes.summary: Returns the access-gate password cached in sessionStorage, or null when none is stored.

nodes.name: setStoredPassword
nodes.summary: Persists the access-gate password to sessionStorage for the current tab lifetime.

nodes.name: clearStoredPassword
nodes.summary: Removes the cached access-gate password from sessionStorage after a 401 or sign-out.

nodes.name: AccessDeniedError
nodes.summary: Custom Error subclass thrown by token clients when the backend rejects a request with HTTP 401, so UI can prompt for the gate password.

nodes.name: liveKitRemoteRoomSession.ts
nodes.summary: Orchestrates a LiveKit remote room session: joins via a minted room token, manages local mic/camera tracks, subscribes to the remote participant's audio/video, and hands the original audio stream to a realtime translation session.
nodes.languageNotes: Single large factory function exposes a controller object with start/stop/setMicrophoneEnabled/setCameraEnabled/setLocalYouHear hooks; relies heavily on LiveKit RoomEvent listeners.

nodes.name: createLiveKitRemoteRoomSession
nodes.summary: Factory that wires a LiveKit Room with token minting, local mic/camera control, remote participant tracking, and realtime translation handoff, returning a controller for UI use.

nodes.name: realtimeTokenClient.ts
nodes.summary: Browser client that POSTs to the backend /realtime/token endpoint, attaching the access-gate password and validating the response with shared Zod schemas before returning a short-lived OpenAI realtime client secret.

nodes.name: RealtimeTokenClientError
nodes.summary: Typed error thrown when the realtime token request fails validation, network, or backend-level checks, carrying an error code and optional status.

nodes.name: requestRealtimeToken
nodes.summary: Validates the request, calls the backend /realtime/token endpoint with the access-gate password, handles 401/timeout/error cases, and returns a Zod-validated realtime token response.

nodes.name: realtimeTranslationSession.ts
nodes.summary: Owns the browser-side WebRTC realtime translation session: captures or accepts a mic stream, exchanges SDP directly with the OpenAI realtime endpoint using a minted client secret, surfaces transcript deltas, and plays back translated audio.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses AbortSignal-aware `abortable` helper to make every async step cancellable without leaking peer connections or tracks.

nodes.name: RealtimeTranslationSessionError
nodes.summary: Typed error raised by the realtime translation session for SDP, network, or media failures so callers can react appropriately.

nodes.name: parseTranscriptDelta
nodes.summary: Best-effort parser for realtime data-channel messages that extracts the transcript text and isFinal flag from OpenAI realtime delta events.

nodes.name: abortable
nodes.summary: Wraps a promise so it rejects with the AbortSignal's reason if aborted mid-flight, used to make SDP/media setup steps cancellable.

nodes.name: createRealtimeTranslationSession
nodes.summary: Factory that builds an RTCPeerConnection, attaches the local/forwarded mic stream, exchanges an SDP offer with the OpenAI realtime endpoint, and returns a controller that exposes stop and audio enable/disable.

nodes.name: roomTokenClient.ts
nodes.summary: Browser client for the backend room API: creates new LiveKit rooms and mints scoped room tokens, attaching the access-gate password and validating responses with shared Zod schemas.

nodes.name: RoomTokenClientError
nodes.summary: Typed error thrown by room create/token requests carrying an error code and optional HTTP status for UI handling.

nodes.name: parseApiError
nodes.summary: Reads a non-2xx response body, validates it against the shared apiErrorSchema, and returns a RoomTokenClientError preserving the backend's error code or a fallback.

nodes.name: requestRoomCreate
nodes.summary: Calls POST /rooms with the access-gate password, handles 401 by clearing the cached password, and returns a Zod-validated roomCreateResponse.

nodes.name: requestRoomToken
nodes.summary: Validates roomId and request, calls POST /rooms/:id/token, handles 401, and returns a Zod-validated room access token for joining the LiveKit room.

nodes.name: accessGate.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest unit tests for sessionStorage password helpers and the AccessDeniedError constructor in accessGate.

nodes.name: liveKitRemoteRoomSession.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest unit tests that drive createLiveKitRemoteRoomSession against a fake LiveKit Room, verifying token minting, mic/camera control, remote participant handling, and translation handoff.

nodes.name: createFakeRoom
nodes.summary: Test helper that builds a minimal fake LiveKit Room with vi.fn mocks for connect/disconnect/setMicrophoneEnabled/setCameraEnabled and a handler map for RoomEvent subscriptions.

nodes.name: realtimeTokenClient.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest unit tests covering validation, gate-password headers, 401 clearing, timeout handling, and schema validation in requestRealtimeToken.

nodes.name: realtimeTranslationSession.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest unit tests for createRealtimeTranslationSession using fake MediaStream, RTCPeerConnection and DataChannel doubles to verify SDP exchange, transcript delta parsing, abort handling, and teardown.

nodes.name: roomTokenClient.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest unit tests for room create/token requests covering header injection, 401 password clearing, error parsing, and schema validation.

nodes.name: App.tsx
nodes.summary: Top-level React component that orchestrates the entire SimTalk experience: lobby selection, access gate, practice/turnabout/listener/remote-room session lifecycles, transcript buffers, MediaRecorder capture, and routing between screens.
nodes.languageNotes: Single large function component with extensive useState/useRef/useCallback hooks; manages multiple async session subsystems (OpenAI Realtime, LiveKit, recorder) from one place.

nodes.name: VideoTile.tsx
nodes.summary: Branded video tile that attaches a LiveKit VideoTrack to a media element and overlays display name, language pill, mic state, and a speaking indicator for remote-room participants.

nodes.name: languages.ts
nodes.summary: Authoritative SimTalk language registry exposing the AUTO sentinel, LANGUAGES array (BCP-47 tags + display metadata), and lookup helpers used across screens, session code, and tests.

nodes.name: ListenerSurface.tsx
nodes.summary: Listener-mode screen that renders the latest translated sentences, scrolling history, and a pause/transcript control bar for one-way listening sessions.

nodes.name: RemoteRoomSurface.tsx
nodes.summary: Remote-room screen that renders the local and remote LiveKit video tiles, share-link controls, language pickers, caption banner, and call controls for a two-participant translated WebRTC session.

nodes.name: Summary.tsx
nodes.summary: Post-session summary screen showing source/translated transcripts, per-turn bubbles, optional audio playback, and copy/download/new-session actions.

nodes.name: TurnaboutSurface.tsx
nodes.summary: Turnabout-mode screen rendering bilingual turn bubbles, speaker badges, and a press-and-hold push-to-talk mic control that drives the active-side translation flow.

nodes.name: SessionHeader.tsx
nodes.summary: Sticky session header showing mode, language pair, active speaker, live elapsed timer, status indicator, and exit/dev-drawer controls during an active translation session.

nodes.name: main.tsx
nodes.summary: Vite entry point that mounts the React App component into #root inside StrictMode and loads the global design tokens stylesheet.

nodes.name: RemoteRoomSurface.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Component test for RemoteRoomSurface verifying that the share link, language pickers, and join/leave callbacks render and behave correctly in idle and joined states.

nodes.name: roomIdFromPathname
nodes.summary: Parses the current window pathname for a `/rooms/<id>` segment and returns the decoded room id, or null when the route is not a remote-room URL.

nodes.name: App
nodes.summary: Root React component that wires lobby/practice/turnabout/listener/remote-room views together, owns transcript state, manages access-gate flow, and drives realtime translation and LiveKit session lifecycles.
nodes.languageNotes: Encapsulates ~40 useState/useRef/useCallback hooks and several useEffect cleanup handlers in a single function — refactor candidate.

nodes.name: Pill
nodes.summary: Internal styled pill primitive used inside VideoTile to render small status badges (display name, language) over the video feed.

nodes.name: VideoTile
nodes.summary: Renders a participant's video tile, attaching the given LiveKit VideoTrack via a ref-driven useEffect, with overlaid display name, language picker, mic toggle, speaking indicator, and waiting-state placeholder.

nodes.name: findLanguage
nodes.summary: Returns the LANGUAGES entry whose BCP-47 tag matches the given string, or undefined when not present.

nodes.name: isAutoLanguage
nodes.summary: Type-guard returning true when the given language is the AUTO sentinel used to enable auto-detection of the source language.

nodes.name: ListenerSurface
nodes.summary: Renders the listener-mode UI: target-language header, live translated sentences derived from the streaming buffer, recent history, and pause/transcript actions.

nodes.name: PageShell
nodes.summary: Internal layout wrapper providing the SimTalk nebula background, vertical flex stack, and responsive max-width container for the remote-room screen.

nodes.name: Header
nodes.summary: Renders the branded remote-room header strip with the SimTalk logotype, live indicator, and a Beta pill.

nodes.name: RemoteRoomSurface
nodes.summary: Two-participant remote-room screen: renders local/remote video tiles, share-link copy control, source/target language pickers, caption banner, audio mute, and join/leave call controls for a translated LiveKit session.

nodes.name: Summary
nodes.summary: Post-session summary view rendering source and translated transcripts, per-turn bubbles, an optional audio player, and copy/download/new-session call-to-action buttons.

nodes.name: SpeakerBadge
nodes.summary: Speaker badge showing a language label, optional auto indicator, and a live-dot animation when the represented side is actively speaking.

nodes.name: Bubble
nodes.summary: Renders a single turnabout turn as paired source + translated bubbles, with placeholders for empty sides and visual treatments per speaker side.

nodes.name: TurnaboutSurface
nodes.summary: Renders the turnabout chat thread of bilingual bubbles and a press-and-hold push-to-talk mic that fires onMicDown/onMicUp with pointer capture handling.

nodes.name: SessionHeader
nodes.summary: Sticky in-session header that displays the mode and language pair, ticks a 1s elapsed timer, surfaces the current status, and exposes exit and dev-drawer toggle buttons.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Single source of truth for cross-boundary contracts: Zod schemas and inferred TypeScript types for realtime token, LiveKit room create/token, API errors, health, and translation modes shared by the backend and frontend.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Zod for runtime validation and exports inferred TypeScript types via z.infer so request/response shapes stay synchronized.

nodes.name: App.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Comprehensive Vitest component test suite for the frontend App, mocking fetch, MediaRecorder, and RTCPeerConnection to exercise the access gate flow, token minting, LiveKit room provisioning, and translation session lifecycle.
nodes.languageNotes: Stubs WebRTC and MediaRecorder browser APIs via vi.stubGlobal to drive realistic UI flows without a real browser audio pipeline.

nodes.name: index.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest unit tests covering shared Zod schemas in shared/types — validates conversation modes, language codes, token request/response shapes, room creation, and API error envelopes.

nodes.name: ci.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions CI workflow that runs on every push and PR, executing format, lint, typecheck, unit tests, build, and Playwright end-to-end tests on Node 22 with pnpm 10.16.1.
nodes.languageNotes: Pins third-party actions by full commit SHA and disables persist-credentials on checkout for supply-chain hardening.

nodes.name: vercel-deploy.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions deployment workflow gated on CI success that publishes Vercel preview builds for non-main pushes and production builds for main, using protected environments with Vercel CLI pull/build/deploy.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses workflow_run trigger with branch-based conditional jobs and environment-scoped secrets to separate preview from production deploys.

nodes.name: CODEMAP.json
nodes.summary: Generated repository codemap consumed by the Agentic OS routing engine; enumerates every project entry under schema_version/generated_at/root/entries so route_task.py can map task descriptions to relevant files.
nodes.languageNotes: Large auto-generated JSON (~4k lines); should be refreshed via scripts/agentic tooling rather than hand-edited.

nodes.name: MEMORY_INDEX.md
nodes.summary: Top-level Agentic OS memory index that catalogues architecture rules, subsystem rules, decisions, incidents, lessons, high-risk areas, and routing hints used to bias task routing.

nodes.name: PROJECT_BRIEF.md
nodes.summary: Agent-facing project brief summarising SimTalk's purpose, stack, high-level architecture, deployment model, major subsystems, source-of-truth files, key constraints, and open unknowns.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Index for the Agentic OS subsystem briefs, defining the required structure of each subsystem file and enumerating SimTalk's five subsystems (web, api, shared, tests, infra).

nodes.name: api.md
nodes.summary: Subsystem brief for the Hono backend: documents its token-minting purpose, source-of-truth files, public token/health contracts, security invariants (no audio proxy, restricted CORS, no key leakage), and do-not-do rules.

nodes.name: infra.md
nodes.summary: Subsystem brief for deployment and CI: documents Vercel Phase 1 and planned Cloud Run Phase 2 targets, environment variable contracts, secret-handling invariants, and CI gating rules.

nodes.name: shared.md
nodes.summary: Subsystem brief for the cross-package shared types package: documents purpose as the single source of truth for API contracts, mode/language enums, and the rule that shared code must be pure and isomorphic.

nodes.name: tests.md
nodes.summary: Subsystem brief for the Playwright E2E suite: documents scope, the deterministic-stack and no-live-OpenAI invariants, common WebRTC/mic flakiness pitfalls, and rules against committing real audio fixtures.

nodes.name: web.md
nodes.summary: Subsystem brief for the React/Vite frontend: documents WebRTC session orchestration, access-gate UX, key source files, invariants (no API key in browser, no audio sent to backend, opt-in recording), and per-mode behaviour.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Repo-orientation guide for AI coding assistants, summarising the pnpm workspace layout, commands, three-boundary realtime translation architecture, hard Phase 1 invariants, and frontend conventions.

nodes.name: PRD.md
nodes.summary: Product Requirements Document defining SimTalk's vision, target users, MVP/Phase 2 scope, success metrics, non-goals, key risks, and product principles for the realtime translation app.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Primary project README covering status, tech stack, architecture, security/privacy, project structure, local development, environment variables, API usage, testing, Vercel deployment, standards, roadmap, and decision log.

nodes.name: System_Architecture.md
nodes.summary: System architecture specification describing the Phase 1 token-minting backend and direct browser-to-OpenAI Realtime flow, plus Phase 1.5 private rooms and Phase 2 multi-user LiveKit architecture.

nodes.name: agentic_os_plan_v2.md
nodes.summary: In-depth manifesto and reference implementation plan for the Agentic OS — the in-repo memory/routing system under .agentic/ and scripts/agentic/ — covering principles, skills, tooling, repo structure, phased rollout, and design guardrails.

nodes.name: components.json
nodes.summary: shadcn/ui generator configuration (style, Tailwind, RSC, TSX, path aliases, icon library); per CLAUDE.md this is a stale scaffold remnant since Tailwind and shadcn are not actually installed in the active frontend.
nodes.languageNotes: Tracked but unused — flagged stale by CLAUDE.md alongside frontend/src/components/ui/ and frontend/src/lib/utils.ts.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Root workspace manifest pinning pnpm 10 and Node 22 engines, defining repo-level dev/build/test/typecheck/e2e scripts that fan out across the shared-types, backend, and frontend packages.

nodes.name: pnpm-workspace.yaml
nodes.summary: pnpm workspace declaration enumerating the three packages (backend, frontend, shared/types) that participate in the monorepo.

nodes.name: tsconfig.base.json
nodes.summary: Shared TypeScript base config establishing strict mode and common compiler options inherited by the backend, frontend, and shared-types package tsconfigs.

nodes.name: vercel.json
nodes.summary: Vercel deployment configuration setting the build/install commands, Vite framework, frontend output directory, SPA rewrites, and required security response headers (CSP, HSTS, frame/MIME/referrer policies).

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Template documenting all backend runtime environment variables: OpenAI realtime credentials, LiveKit room credentials, allowed origins, rate-limit windows, and the optional app access password.
nodes.languageNotes: Eighteen KEY=VALUE entries spanning OpenAI realtime, LiveKit, CORS, rate-limit, and access-gate settings — safe template counterpart to backend/.env.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Manifest for the @simtalk/backend workspace package; declares Hono, @hono/node-server, livekit-server-sdk, and the shared-types workspace dep, plus dev/build/test scripts (tsx watch, tsc, vitest).

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: Production TypeScript compiler config for the backend; extends the repo base, targets ES2022 NodeNext modules, emits declarations and source maps from src/ to dist/, and excludes test files.

nodes.name: tsconfig.test.json
nodes.summary: Test-only TypeScript config that extends backend/tsconfig.json, adds DOM lib and Vitest path aliases, includes the repo-level tests/backend tree, and runs in noEmit type-check mode.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses paths aliases to resolve @simtalk/shared-types directly to the workspace source so type-checking does not require a prior shared-types build.

nodes.name: 00_repo_context.md
nodes.summary: Auto-generated repository context snapshot covering git state, project state, source files reviewed, and configuration/environment for the SimTalk codebase.

nodes.name: 01_architecture_map.md
nodes.summary: Auto-generated architecture and tech-stack map describing runtime flows, entrypoints, storage, tests/CI, and remaining uncertainties for the realtime translation system.

nodes.name: 02_folder_structure.md
nodes.summary: Auto-generated folder-structure map of the monorepo and notable drift between expected and actual layout.

nodes.name: 03_file_index.md
nodes.summary: Auto-generated index categorising every tracked source/config/test/script file across root docs, the Vercel adapter, backend, shared types, frontend, tests, and Agentic OS tooling.

nodes.name: 04_readme_gap_analysis.md
nodes.summary: Auto-generated README gap analysis classifying the project, auditing consistency/completeness, listing required README updates, and noting claims to avoid.

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Documents the single VITE_API_BASE_URL environment variable the frontend needs to reach the Hono backend at runtime.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Vite HTML entry that mounts the React app into #root, declares favicons, SimTalk OpenGraph/Twitter metadata, and preloads Poppins/Luckiest Guy fonts.
nodes.languageNotes: Vite expects index.html at the project root; the module script tag points at src/main.tsx as the SPA entry.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Frontend package manifest for @simtalk/frontend declaring React 19 + Vite 7 + LiveKit deps, the workspace dependency on @simtalk/shared-types, and dev/build/test scripts.

nodes.name: tsconfig.app.json
nodes.summary: Application TypeScript project extending the repo base config, enabling react-jsx and pulling in vite/client and vitest/globals ambient types over the src tree.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: Root TypeScript solution file with no own files that references the app and node sub-projects for a single tsc -b composite build.

nodes.name: tsconfig.node.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript project for Node-side tooling — typechecks vite.config.ts under NodeNext module resolution with @types/node.

nodes.name: tsconfig.test.json
nodes.summary: Test TypeScript project extending the app config, mapping @simtalk/shared-types, React, LiveKit, and testing-library/vitest paths so unit/component tests in tests/frontend/ typecheck without bundling.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses TypeScript path aliases to resolve workspace and node_modules type declarations directly so vitest typechecks without a Vite resolver.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Package manifest for @simtalk/shared-types declaring it a private ESM package that builds Zod schemas/types via tsc, exposes dist/index as the public entry, and depends on zod with vitest/typescript for dev.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript build config for the shared-types package extending the workspace base, emitting declarations and source maps from src/ to dist/ using NodeNext module resolution while excluding tests.

nodes.name: tsconfig.test.json
nodes.summary: Type-check-only TypeScript config for shared-types tests; extends the package tsconfig, includes the repo's tests/shared and vitest.config.ts, and pins vitest module paths for IDE/CI typechecking.

nodes.name: agentic.json
nodes.summary: Central Agentic OS configuration declaring repo paths, ignore globs, test discovery rules, high-risk patterns, and subsystem keyword maps consumed by the routing and memory scripts.

nodes.name: decisions.md
nodes.summary: Architectural decision log capturing Phase 1 choices: private single-device app, React+Vite frontend, Hono backend, OpenAI gpt-realtime-translate, no DB, browser-local recording, Vercel deploy, and backend access gate.

nodes.name: incidents.md
nodes.summary: Empty incident log scaffold with a template header, ready to record production incidents and remediations once they occur.

nodes.name: settings.local.json
nodes.summary: Local Claude Code permission allow-list authorising specific bash commands (pnpm, git, mkdir, ls, cat, tree) for this workspace.

nodes.name: phase_one_implementation_efb87251.plan.md
nodes.summary: Phase 1 coding plan grounding the realtime translation MVP: invariants, unknowns, implementation slices, and recommended first coding pass for the React+Hono stack.

nodes.name: simtalk_deployment_rooms_b4ffca08.plan.md
nodes.summary: Detailed plan for Vercel deployment, security hardening, and Phase 1.5 LiveKit-backed remote rooms — covers CI/CD, env vars, browser test checklist, and approval checkpoints.

nodes.name: skill.md
nodes.summary: Cursor skill describing the agenticOS-context workflow that compiles a focused context bundle via scripts/agentic/route_task.py before any non-trivial coding task.

nodes.name: skill.md
nodes.summary: Cursor skill describing when and how to refresh Agentic OS memory (CODEMAP, lessons, subsystem notes) after durable changes, with anti-bloat rules.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Operating procedure for migrating a repository's tests into a clean /tests/ structure: discovery, classification, proposed structure, migration plan, and verification steps.

nodes.name: .prettierignore
nodes.summary: Prettier ignore list excluding build artefacts, generated dirs, and lockfiles from formatting.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Understand-Anything ignore list narrowing the codebase scan by excluding generated, vendored, and tooling-only paths.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Vercel serverless function entry point that re-exports the Hono `app` instance from backend/src/app.ts so the same router serves both local and Vercel deployments.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler configuration for the Vercel `api/` entry, extending the backend tsconfig and limiting includes to the api directory.

nodes.name: app.ts
nodes.summary: Hono application factory that wires access-gate, CORS, security-headers, health, realtime token, and rooms routes plus 404/error handlers using validated config and dependencies.

nodes.name: createApp
nodes.summary: Builds the Hono app instance: applies access gate, CORS, security headers, mounts health/realtime/rooms routes, and installs JSON-validated 404 and error responders.

nodes.name: config.ts
nodes.summary: Builds the validated application config from process env: parses ports, allowed origins, access passwords, rate-limit windows, OpenAI realtime tunables, and LiveKit credentials.

nodes.name: parseIntegerInRange
nodes.summary: Parses an env value as an integer, falls back to a default, and clamps the result to the supplied [min, max] range.

nodes.name: createAppConfig
nodes.summary: Reads the env record and produces the immutable AppConfig used by the Hono app, validating required secrets and applying safe defaults for optional knobs.

nodes.name: accessGate.ts
nodes.summary: Hono middleware that gates protected routes on an `x-access-password` header, comparing SHA-256 hashes with timingSafeEqual against the configured password list.

nodes.name: createAccessGateMiddleware
nodes.summary: Factory returning a Hono middleware that enforces the access password header against pre-hashed allowed values using constant-time comparison.

nodes.name: cors.ts
nodes.summary: Strict origin-reflecting CORS middleware that only sets Access-Control-Allow-* headers when the request Origin is in the configured allow-list and short-circuits OPTIONS preflights.

nodes.name: createCorsMiddleware
nodes.summary: Builds a Hono middleware that conditionally reflects allow-listed origins, advertises permitted methods and headers, and handles OPTIONS preflight short-circuits.

nodes.name: rateLimit.ts
nodes.summary: In-memory fixed-window rate limiter for Hono routes, keyed by client IP from x-forwarded-for/x-real-ip with bounded entry pruning to cap memory usage.

nodes.name: clientKeyFromHeaders
nodes.summary: Derives the client identifier from x-forwarded-for (first non-empty token) or x-real-ip, falling back to the literal 'unknown'.

nodes.name: pruneEntries
nodes.summary: Evicts expired buckets and, if still above maxEntries, drops the oldest insertion-ordered keys to keep the rate-limit map bounded.

nodes.name: createRateLimitMiddleware
nodes.summary: Factory returning a Hono middleware enforcing maxRequests per windowMs per client, emitting Retry-After and a Zod-validated 429 JSON body when exceeded.

nodes.name: securityHeaders.ts
nodes.summary: Lightweight middleware that appends baseline security headers (CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy, X-Content-Type-Options) to every response.

nodes.name: securityHeaders
nodes.summary: Hono middleware that calls next then sets the standard security header bundle on the outgoing response.

nodes.name: health.ts
nodes.summary: Tiny Hono sub-app exposing GET /health with a Zod-validated status payload for liveness probes.

nodes.name: realtime.ts
nodes.summary: Hono sub-app for POST /realtime/token: rate-limited, Zod-validated request that mints a short-lived OpenAI realtime client secret via the OpenAiRealtime service.

nodes.name: createRealtimeRoute
nodes.summary: Builds the realtime token Hono route, wiring rate-limit middleware, JSON parsing, Zod validation, the OpenAiRealtime service call, and uniform error responses with request IDs.

nodes.name: rooms.ts
nodes.summary: Hono sub-app for POST /rooms (create LiveKit room) and POST /rooms/:roomId/token (mint participant token), with rate-limit middleware and Zod-validated request/response payloads.

nodes.name: createRoomsRoute
nodes.summary: Constructs the rooms Hono router: applies rate-limit, defines room creation and participant-token endpoints, validates input/output via shared Zod schemas, and translates service errors into clean API errors.

nodes.name: server.ts
nodes.summary: Backend entry point that loads config, instantiates the Hono app via createApp, and serves it through @hono/node-server on the configured port.

nodes.name: liveKitRooms.ts
nodes.summary: LiveKit room service: lazily constructs a RoomServiceClient, idempotently ensures rooms exist, mints JWT participant tokens with publish/subscribe grants, and surfaces typed LiveKitRoomError failures.

nodes.name: LiveKitRoomError
nodes.summary: Typed Error subclass carrying an HTTP-friendly status code and machine code so route handlers can map LiveKit failures to API error responses.

nodes.name: assertLiveKitConfig
nodes.summary: Validates that LiveKit URL/key/secret are present in the AppConfig, throwing LiveKitRoomError(503,'livekit_not_configured') when any are missing.

nodes.name: defaultTokenSigner
nodes.summary: Default JWT signer that builds a LiveKit AccessToken with publish/subscribe grants for the supplied room and participant identity, expiring after ttlSeconds.

nodes.name: createLiveKitRoomService
nodes.summary: Factory that returns a LiveKit room service exposing createRoom and createParticipantToken, idempotent over already-exists errors and producing schema-validated response shapes.

nodes.name: openAiRealtime.ts
nodes.summary: OpenAI Realtime service: posts to the client-secret endpoint with abortable timeout, sanitises errors into typed OpenAiRealtimeError, and parses responses into the shared realtime token schema.

nodes.name: OpenAiRealtimeError
nodes.summary: Typed Error subclass carrying status, code, and optional details so realtime route handlers can return sanitised API errors without leaking upstream payloads.

nodes.name: unixSecondsToIso
nodes.summary: Converts a finite unix-seconds timestamp into an ISO-8601 string, returning undefined for missing or non-finite inputs.

nodes.name: parseOpenAiClientSecretResponse
nodes.summary: Validates the OpenAI client-secret response payload and projects it onto the shared RealtimeTokenResponse shape, including ISO-formatted issued/expires timestamps.

nodes.name: createOpenAiRealtimeService
nodes.summary: Factory returning a service whose createTranslationClientSecret method posts to OpenAI with an abortable timeout, classifies non-2xx responses into OpenAiRealtimeError, and returns the parsed token response.

nodes.name: vitest.config.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest configuration for the Hono backend that aliases the @simtalk/shared-types package and discovers tests under tests/backend.

nodes.name: 2026-05-24-access-gate.md
nodes.summary: Step-by-step implementation plan for the Phase-1 access-gate feature, covering the shared error code, backend middleware, frontend storage helper, header injection, modal, and end-to-end verification.

nodes.name: 2026-05-26-they-speak-auto-default.md
nodes.summary: Implementation plan for the THEY SPEAK auto-default and partner-language mirroring feature, splitting work across LiveKit session helpers, App.tsx wiring, RemoteRoomSurface and VideoTile.

nodes.name: 2026-05-24-access-gate-design.md
nodes.summary: Design specification for the Phase-1 access gate covering protected endpoints, backend middleware, frontend storage and modal flow, threat model, and testing strategy.

nodes.name: 2026-05-26-they-speak-auto-default-design.md
nodes.summary: Design spec for partner-language mirroring via LiveKit participant attributes, describing session helpers, App-level wiring, and UI changes for the THEY SPEAK auto picker.

nodes.name: ux-redesign-plan.md
nodes.summary: SimTalk Phase-1 UX simplification plan: information architecture, screen hierarchy, mode-specific flows, design tokens, and an incremental PR roadmap for the redesign.

nodes.name: eslint.config.js
nodes.summary: Flat ESLint configuration enabling the recommended JS and TypeScript rule sets plus react-hooks rules for all .ts/.tsx files, with ignore patterns for build, coverage, and tooling directories.

nodes.name: site.webmanifest
nodes.summary: PWA web app manifest declaring the SimTalk app name, standalone display mode, navy theme colors, and Android home-screen icons.

nodes.name: tokens.css
nodes.summary: SimTalk design-system stylesheet defining brand color, typography, radius, spacing, and shadow CSS custom properties plus the signature nebula gradient consumed by every frontend component.

nodes.name: vite.config.ts
nodes.summary: Vite configuration for the React 19 frontend wiring the @vitejs/plugin-react plugin, monorepo alias resolution (@, @simtalk/shared-types, react), dev/preview ports, and Vitest jsdom test setup pointing at tests/frontend.

nodes.name: playwright.config.ts
nodes.summary: Playwright end-to-end configuration targeting tests/e2e against the Vite dev server on 127.0.0.1:5173, with a Chromium project and CI-aware retry/reporter behaviour.

nodes.name: build_codemap.py
nodes.summary: Agentic OS codemap builder that walks the repo, applies ignore globs, detects language/subsystem/risk tags per file, finds related tests, and writes a sorted .agentic/CODEMAP.json inventory.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Short reference for the local Agentic OS Python tooling describing build_codemap, route_task, validate_memory, and update_memory, plus the rule that they only touch .agentic and scripts/agentic.

nodes.name: route_task.py
nodes.summary: Agentic OS routing engine that scores CODEMAP entries against a task description, applies explicit file/path overrides, selects subsystem context, computes confidence, and writes a context bundle to .agentic/CONTEXT.

nodes.name: test_build_codemap.py
nodes.summary: Unittest suite covering _is_default_ignored's path-segment matching to ensure multi-segment default-ignore entries are respected without false positives on individual segments.

nodes.name: test_route_task.py
nodes.summary: Unittest suite covering keyword-part matching and end-to-end route_task bundle behaviour including explicit path/basename overrides and filesystem fallback caps.

nodes.name: test_update_memory.py
nodes.summary: Unittest suite for update_memory covering JSON loader error paths and stale-subsystem detection that distinguishes planned stubs from drift relative to the CODEMAP.

nodes.name: test_validate_memory.py
nodes.summary: Unittest suite for validate_memory's curated-path-reference checker, verifying that external URLs are ignored and relative markdown links resolve against the markdown file or repo root.

nodes.name: update_memory.py
nodes.summary: Agentic OS refresh script that rebuilds the CODEMAP, compares detected subsystems against curated SUBSYSTEMS files, and prints planned/stale/missing subsystem summaries to guide manual updates.

nodes.name: validate_memory.py
nodes.summary: Agentic OS validator that checks required memory files exist, validates the CODEMAP shape, and warns when curated markdown references point to missing paths.

nodes.name: vitest.config.ts
nodes.summary: Minimal Vitest configuration for the shared-types package that runs spec files under the repo-root tests/shared directory.

nodes.name: accessGate.test.ts
nodes.summary: Backend integration tests that mount the Hono app with a stubbed RoomService and verify the access-gate middleware enforces APP_ACCESS_PASSWORD on /realtime/token and /rooms.

nodes.name: app.test.ts
nodes.summary: Backend smoke integration tests asserting that createApp wires CORS, security headers, the health route, and a 404 fallback on unknown paths.

nodes.name: realtime.test.ts
nodes.summary: Integration tests for POST /realtime/token covering validation, OpenAI client-secret success, upstream error sanitization, and rate-limiter behaviour using stubbed fetch and configurable AppConfig.

nodes.name: rooms.test.ts
nodes.summary: Integration tests for the /rooms routes covering token issuance, room metadata fetches, validation failures, and LiveKit service error propagation against a stubbed RoomService.

nodes.name: _matches_any
nodes.summary: Tests whether a normalized path matches any of the supplied glob patterns, supporting double-star prefixes, anchored, and fnmatch-style segments.

nodes.name: _detect_subsystem
nodes.summary: Maps a repo-relative path to the best-fit subsystem name by matching configured keyword prefixes against path segments.

nodes.name: _detect_risk_tags
nodes.summary: Annotates a codemap entry with risk tags by matching its path against configured risky-pattern globs.

nodes.name: _related_tests
nodes.summary: Finds candidate test files for a given source path by matching configured test globs against the basename across the discovered file list.

nodes.name: _walk
nodes.summary: Walks the repository tree from a root, pruning default- and configured-ignored directories and returning sorted relative directory and file path lists.

nodes.name: _build_entries
nodes.summary: Builds the sorted CODEMAP entries list by classifying each discovered directory and file with language, subsystem, risk tags, related tests, and keyword hints.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Entry point that loads the agentic config, walks the repo, builds entries, and writes the timestamped CODEMAP.json under .agentic.

nodes.name: _contains_explicit_reference
nodes.summary: Checks whether a normalized task description literally mentions a given file reference using anchored regex matching on word boundaries.

nodes.name: _explicit_file_reference_matches
nodes.summary: Scans CODEMAP entries for files whose path or basename is explicitly named in the task, returning matching entries sorted by path.

nodes.name: _explicit_files_from_filesystem
nodes.summary: Falls back to walking the working tree to find files explicitly named in a task when the CODEMAP misses them, skipping build/cache/venv directories and capping results.

nodes.name: _score_entry
nodes.summary: Computes a routing score for a CODEMAP entry by combining tag, keyword, subsystem, and risk-tag matches against the task tokens.

nodes.name: _routable_entries_for_selection
nodes.summary: Expands a directory CODEMAP entry into the file entries it owns so directory hits still surface concrete routable files.

nodes.name: _confidence
nodes.summary: Derives a textual confidence label (high/medium/low) plus stop conditions from the selection size, subsystems found, test coverage, and explicit-path signals.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point: parses the task argv, loads config and CODEMAP, scores and selects entries with explicit overrides, attaches memory files, computes confidence, and writes the context bundle JSON.

nodes.name: _planned_subsystem_files
nodes.summary: Scans existing SUBSYSTEMS markdown files for a 'planned' marker so curated stubs aren't flagged as stale by the drift checker.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that re-runs build_codemap, then prints missing, stale, and planned subsystem files based on detected vs. curated coverage.

nodes.name: _looks_like_path
nodes.summary: Heuristic that decides whether a token extracted from markdown is plausibly a filesystem path based on shape and recognised extensions.

nodes.name: _check_codemap
nodes.summary: Loads the CODEMAP JSON, asserts schema invariants, and emits warnings for entries whose path no longer exists on disk.

nodes.name: _check_curated_path_refs
nodes.summary: Parses curated markdown memory files, extracts plausible path references, and warns when they don't resolve relative to the markdown file or repo root.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that verifies required memory files exist, runs CODEMAP and curated-path checks, prints summarized findings, and exits non-zero on failures.

nodes.name: DefaultIgnorePartsTest
nodes.summary: Unit tests asserting _is_default_ignored matches multi-segment ignore entries by full path and not by individual path components.

nodes.name: KeywordPartMatchesTest
nodes.summary: Unit tests for _keyword_part_matches, ensuring short keyword prefixes do not match unrelated tokens while exact matches and limited word-form aliases still resolve.

nodes.name: RouteTaskBundleTest
nodes.summary: End-to-end tests for the route_task bundle covering explicit path and basename overrides, function-name hints, filesystem fallback exclusions, and selected-path capping.

nodes.name: LoadJsonTest
nodes.summary: Unit tests verifying _load_json raises FileNotFoundError and JSONDecodeError on missing or malformed inputs.

nodes.name: StaleSubsystemDetectionTest
nodes.summary: Integration-style tests that build fixture memory files and confirm planned subsystem stubs are not reported as stale while genuinely unsupported subsystems are.

nodes.name: CuratedPathRefsTest
nodes.summary: Tests that _check_curated_path_refs ignores external URLs, resolves relative markdown references from the markdown file, and falls back to the repo root when needed.

nodes.name: createRoomService
nodes.summary: Test helper that builds a stub RoomService whose issueToken and getRoomInfo methods return deterministic LiveKit-shaped responses via vi.fn mocks.

nodes.name: realtimeRequest
nodes.summary: Test helper that constructs a POST /realtime/token Request with optional headers and a default valid TokenRequest body for access-gate assertions.

nodes.name: createTestConfig
nodes.summary: Builds an AppConfig for realtime route tests, merging fixture defaults (OpenAI key, allowed origins, rate-limit knobs) with optional overrides.

nodes.name: createTokenRequest
nodes.summary: Constructs a POST /realtime/token Request with a default valid TokenRequest body and optional header overrides for the integration suite.

nodes.name: createRoomService
nodes.summary: Test helper that builds a stub RoomService for the /rooms route tests, returning deterministic issueToken and getRoomInfo responses via vi.fn mocks.

nodes.name: vercelAdapter.test.ts
nodes.summary: Integration test for the Vercel serverless adapter, asserting the exported handler module surface and Hono app wiring.

nodes.name: config.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the backend configuration loader, validating required env handling, defaults, and Zod-based parsing of OpenAI/LiveKit settings.

nodes.name: accessGate.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the password-based access gate middleware, exercising 401/200 paths and ensuring protected routes require valid credentials.

nodes.name: liveKitRooms.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the LiveKit room/token service, verifying room name derivation, identity assignment, and JWT minting against shared config.

nodes.name: openAiRealtime.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the OpenAI realtime client-secret minting service, mocking fetch responses and asserting request shape, sanitized errors, and cache headers.

nodes.name: home.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Playwright end-to-end test for the home/lobby flow, stubbing the realtime token endpoint and browser WebRTC/audio APIs to validate translation session start/stop UX.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses page.addInitScript to inject browser-side mocks for MediaDevices, RTCPeerConnection, and AudioContext so the WebRTC pipeline is fully deterministic in CI.

nodes.name: setupTests.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest global setup for frontend tests, wiring jest-dom matchers and shared testing-library configuration.

nodes.name: createTestConfig
nodes.summary: Test helper that builds an AppConfig with sensible LiveKit/OpenAI defaults and shallow overrides for individual specs.

nodes.name: createTestConfig
nodes.summary: Test helper that constructs an AppConfig fixture for OpenAI realtime service tests, with overridable fields for env-driven scenarios.

nodes.name: mockRealtimeTokenRoute
nodes.summary: Installs a Playwright route handler that intercepts POSTs to /realtime/token, records request payloads, and fulfills with a fake client secret response.

nodes.name: installBrowserRealtimeMocks
nodes.summary: Injects browser-side stubs (MediaDevices.getUserMedia, RTCPeerConnection, AudioContext, MediaRecorder) and OpenAI SDP route mocks so the e2e suite can drive the realtime translation flow without real WebRTC.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses page.addInitScript so the mocks run before any application JS, then patches navigator.mediaDevices and the WebRTC constructors via Object.defineProperty.

layers.name: Frontend UI
layers.description: React 19 + Vite browser UI: SimTalk brand primitives, screens (Lobby, surfaces, Summary, AccessGateModal, TranscriptSheet), session shell components, design tokens, app entry, HTML host, and the frontend package's build/runtime configuration.

layers.name: Realtime Session Client
layers.description: Browser-side orchestration that mints credentials and runs translated WebRTC/LiveKit sessions directly against OpenAI and LiveKit, plus the access-gate flow that fronts every session.

layers.name: Backend Token Service
layers.description: Hono token-minting backend that issues short-lived OpenAI Realtime client secrets and LiveKit room JWTs without ever proxying audio, exposed through a local Node entry and the Vercel serverless adapter, with its CORS/access-gate/rate-limit/security-headers middleware and per-package build configuration.

layers.name: Shared Contracts
layers.description: @simtalk/shared-types package: Zod schemas and inferred TypeScript types that are the source of truth for every cross-boundary payload (token requests, room requests, modes, language tags, errors, health), plus its TS build and test configuration.

layers.name: Test Suites
layers.description: Repo-root Vitest unit/integration/component suites for backend, frontend, and shared types, the Playwright e2e spec and runner config, and shared test setup harnesses.

layers.name: Deployment & CI/CD
layers.description: Vercel deployment manifest and the two GitHub Actions workflows that run CI checks and ship the app to Vercel.

layers.name: Agentic OS & Tooling
layers.description: In-repo agent operating layer: scripts/agentic Python tools (codemap build, task routing, memory update/validation, their tests), the .agentic memory store (CODEMAP, PROJECT_BRIEF, MEMORY_INDEX, SUBSYSTEMS, LESSONS, CONFIG), Cursor plans and skills, the Understand Anything ignore file, and the agentic OS v2 plan.

layers.name: Project Documentation
layers.description: Human-facing project docs: top-level CLAUDE/PRD/README/System Architecture, the generated docs/_generated repo context bundle, the superpowers plans and specs, and the UX redesign plan.

layers.name: Workspace Configuration
layers.description: Repository-wide configuration that the per-package layers share: pnpm workspace manifest, root package.json, base TypeScript config, ESLint config, the stale shadcn components manifest, the Prettier ignore list, and the local Claude IDE settings.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to understand SimTalk's purpose: a realtime speech-to-speech translation web app built on OpenAI gpt-realtime-translate. The README outlines the three local modes (Listener, Turn-about, Practice), the Phase 1.5 LiveKit two-person remote room, the five runtime boundaries (browser, Hono backend, Vercel API adapter, OpenAI Realtime, LiveKit Cloud), and the hard invariants that govern the design — most importantly that the backend never sees audio or transcripts and never returns the OpenAI API key. Read this before any code so the rest of the tour has a frame to hang on.

tour.title: Architecture and Product Intent
tour.description: Building on the README, the System Architecture spec and PRD ground the technical decisions you will see in the code. They explain why translation runs over WebRTC directly between the browser and OpenAI, why the backend is a token-minting service only, and why audio and transcripts never persist server-side. Skim them now so the boundary choices later in the tour read as deliberate rather than incidental.

tour.title: Shared Contracts (Zod + TypeScript)
tour.description: All cross-boundary payloads are validated against the schemas in `shared/types/src/index.ts`. Modes, language tags, realtime token requests/responses, room requests/responses, API errors, and health responses are defined here as Zod schemas with inferred TypeScript types — making this module the single source of truth for backend-frontend contracts. Every later step that crosses a network boundary references these types.
tour.languageLesson: Zod's `z.infer<typeof Schema>` lets one schema definition produce both runtime validation and a static TypeScript type. The shared-types package builds first in CI so backend and frontend can rely on the compiled `dist/` output.

tour.title: Hono Backend — Server, App, Health
tour.description: `server.ts` is the Node entrypoint that boots `@hono/node-server` against the Hono app. `app.ts` composes the app instance — security headers, CORS, rate limiting, then mounts the route modules. `routes/health.ts` provides the lightweight `GET /health` probe used by Vercel and CI. Together these three files form the skeleton onto which the rest of the backend hangs.

tour.title: Backend Security and Access Layer
tour.description: Every request flows through four middleware modules before reaching a route. `securityHeaders.ts` always sets CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy, and X-Content-Type-Options. `cors.ts` strictly reflects `ALLOWED_ORIGINS` — never `*`. `rateLimit.ts` is the in-memory Phase 1 guardrail on token issuance. `accessGate.ts` enforces the password-protection gate that fronts the prototype, and `config.ts` centralises env-var loading so secret values stay out of route code. These are the project's hard invariants made executable.

tour.title: Token Routes and External Service Adapters
tour.description: This is where the backend's only product responsibility lives: minting short-lived credentials for the browser. `routes/realtime.ts` validates the incoming request against the shared Zod schemas, then delegates to `services/openAiRealtime.ts`, which calls OpenAI's client-secret endpoint using the server-only `OPENAI_API_KEY` and returns a sanitised, browser-safe response. `routes/rooms.ts` and `services/liveKitRooms.ts` do the equivalent for Phase 1.5 LiveKit room creation and participant tokens. Routes stay thin; all external-API logic sits in `services/`.

tour.title: Vercel Adapter and Deployment Wiring
tour.description: `api/index.ts` re-exports the Hono `app` through `@hono/node-server`'s Vercel adapter, so the same code that runs locally under `tsx watch` also runs as a serverless function on Vercel. `vercel.json` rewrites `/api/*` to that adapter and routes everything else to the Vite SPA. This step closes the backend loop — you have now seen how a request enters production, gets validated, and gets a token back.

tour.title: Frontend Boot — Vite, React, App Shell
tour.description: `main.tsx` mounts React 19 into `index.html` and pulls in the brand tokens stylesheet — `tokens.css` defines SimTalk's CSS variable system, semantic colour aliases, base typography, and `prefers-reduced-motion` handling on which every component depends. `App.tsx` is the routing/orchestration hub: it owns mode selection, language pairing, the access-gate flow, and decides which surface (Listener, Turn-about, Practice, Remote Room, Summary) is mounted at any moment. Its high fan-out (17) makes it the natural launching point into the rest of the UI.
tour.languageLesson: Tailwind and shadcn/ui are not installed in the active frontend — `components.json`, `frontend/src/components/ui/`, and `frontend/src/lib/utils.ts` are stale scaffold remnants. The live styling system is the CSS-variable design tokens in `tokens.css`.

tour.title: Browser Realtime Translation Session
tour.description: `accessGate.ts` is the client half of the password gate from Step 5 — it stores and forwards the access secret on token requests. `realtimeTokenClient.ts` POSTs to `/realtime/token` and Zod-validates the response using the contracts from Step 3. `realtimeTranslationSession.ts` then owns the entire WebRTC lifecycle: microphone capture, `RTCPeerConnection`, SDP exchange directly with OpenAI's translation calls URL, remote audio playback, transcript-delta handling, and clean teardown. This module is the heart of the local Listener / Turn-about / Practice flows.

tour.title: LiveKit Remote Room Session (Phase 1.5)
tour.description: Two-person remote rooms reuse the realtime translation engine from Step 9 but with a different audio source. `roomTokenClient.ts` calls `POST /rooms` and `POST /rooms/:roomId/token` (Step 6) to obtain a short-lived participant credential. `liveKitRemoteRoomSession.ts` connects to LiveKit Cloud, publishes the local microphone, subscribes to the remote participant's track, and pipes that subscribed remote stream into the OpenAI translation session — so each participant hears the other already translated. The SimTalk backend never proxies media in this path.

tour.title: UI Surfaces and Brand Primitives
tour.description: These are the screens users actually interact with. `Lobby.tsx` chooses mode and languages. `AccessGateModal.tsx` enforces the password gate visually. `ListenerSurface.tsx`, `TurnaboutSurface.tsx`, and `PracticeSurface.tsx` drive the three local modes; `RemoteRoomSurface.tsx` runs the LiveKit two-person flow; `Summary.tsx` and `TranscriptSheet.tsx` show captured transcripts and downloadable recordings; `SessionHeader.tsx` and `DevDrawer.tsx` provide chrome and developer affordances. They are built on the high-fan-in primitives in `components/brand/`: `primitives.tsx` (buttons, surfaces — fan-in 14), `Icons.tsx` (lucide-derived icon set — fan-in 13), `languages.ts` (the 70+ language catalog — fan-in 11), `LanguagePicker.tsx`, `ModeSegmented.tsx`, and `VideoTile.tsx`. Look here to understand the 8px-rhythm, semantic-token-driven visual system the user rules call out.

tour.title: Tests, CI, and Deployment
tour.description: SimTalk's coverage is broad: Vitest unit and integration suites for every shared contract, backend route/middleware/service, and frontend client/component (the topology analysis paired 8 production modules with their direct unit/component tests), plus Playwright e2e flows that mock the OpenAI and LiveKit network boundaries. `playwright.config.ts` and the per-package `vitest.config.ts` files configure those runners. `.github/workflows/ci.yml` runs them on every push. `.github/workflows/vercel-deploy.yml` is gated on a successful CI run via `workflow_run`, so production deploys cannot ship a red build. CLAUDE.md documents the conventions you should follow when adding to any of this.
tour.languageLesson: GitHub Actions' `workflow_run` trigger lets one workflow gate another. The Vercel deploy job in this repo uses it to require CI to pass first — a cheap way to enforce green-only deploys without paying for a separate environment-protection rule.
